top_signals.title: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet
top_signals.summary: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said today it worked with industry partners to seize hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a sprawling residential proxy service operated by the publicly-traded Israeli company Alarum Technologies [NASDAQ: ALAR]. The action comes roughly two weeks after KrebsOnSecurity published findings from multiple security firms connecting NetNut to the Popa botnet, a collection of at least two million devices that have been compromised by malicious software with little or no consent from victims.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Shipping industry takes major step towards sustainable plastic procurement with new toolkit from the Maritime Association for Clean Seas
top_signals.summary: The Maritime Association for Clean Seas (MACS) has published a series of practical guides to enable the commercial shipping sector to implement sustainable plastic procurement in vessel operations. top_signals.title: DNV Type Approval Design Certificate accelerates deployment of Econowind VentoFoils
top_signals.summary: Econowind has received a Type Approval Design Certificate (TADC) from classification society DNV for its VentoFoil 3-Series boundary layer suction wing. The certification confirms compliance with DNV’s ST-0511 standard for Wind-Assisted Propulsion Systems and enables easier integration of VentoFoils on DNV-classed vessels worldwide. The approval accelerates the deployment of wind propulsion across the shipping industry. ...

top_signals.title: DP World taps Hapag-Lloyd for emissions cuts in Americas
top_signals.summary: Logistics firm DP World will use Hapag-Lloyd’s biofuel bunkering to claim around 4,700 mtCO2e of well-to-wake emissions reductions for its ocean freight operations in the Americas under a book-and-claim arrangement. DP World will book cargo shipments with Hapag-Lloyd in the Americas and claim emissions reductions linked to the use of used cooking oil methyl ester ...

top_signals.title: Asia’s richest nation remains world’s leading maritime center in 2026
top_signals.summary: Singapore, the richest nation in Asia by GDP per capita, has kept its position as the world’s leading maritime center in the 2026 Xinhua-Baltic International Shipping Center Development Index (ISCDI), winning the honor for the 13th consecutive year. The ISCDI Report said the recognition is attributed to Singapore’s strategic location, established maritime services, and importance ...
